mpipe/forward/app.c

Use the gxio_mpipe_ API to forward ethernet packets.

// Copyright 2014 Tilera Corporation. All Rights Reserved.
//
//   The source code contained or described herein and all documents
//   related to the source code ("Material") are owned by Tilera
//   Corporation or its suppliers or licensors.  Title to the Material
//   remains with Tilera Corporation or its suppliers and licensors. The
//   software is licensed under the Tilera MDE License.
//
//   Unless otherwise agreed by Tilera in writing, you may not remove or
//   alter this notice or any other notice embedded in Materials by Tilera
//   or Tilera's suppliers or licensors in any way.
//
//

// ============================================================================
// mpipe/forward/app.c -- mPIPE forwarding example
// ============================================================================

// This example demonstrates using the mPIPE API
// to perform simple forwarding of incoming packets.
//
// Packets are ingressed and egressed over the same network link,
// which defaults to "gbe1" but may be specified via
// the --link command-line argument.

// Standard C includes.
#include <stdio.h>          // printf(), etc.
#include <stdlib.h>         // calloc(), atoi()
#include <stdbool.h>        // bool type and values

// Threading support.
#include <pthread.h>        // pthreads library (-pthread)

// Tilera includes.

// mPIPE API (part of GXIO library, -lgxio).
#include <gxio/mpipe.h>

// Tilera TMC library (-ltmc).
#include <tmc/task.h>       // tmc_task_die()
#include <tmc/cpus.h>       // cpu sets and affinity
#include <tmc/alloc.h>      // memory allocation
#include <tmc/mem.h>        // prefetching
#include <tmc/spin.h>       // spin execution barriers
#include <tmc/sync.h>       // sync execution barriers

// Other Tilera-specific includes.
#include <sys/dataplane.h>  // ZOL (dataplane tile) mode options
#include <arch/chip.h>      // cache line sizes
#include <arch/atomic.h>    // atomic functions
#include <arch/sim.h>       // sim_enable_mpipe_links()


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// constants
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

#define PACKET_BATCH_SIZE 16

#define DEBUG_DATAPLANE

//#define DEBUG_PACKET_CYCLES

//#define DEBUG_INGRESS_QUEUES


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// macros
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

#define ALIGN(p, align) \
  do { (p) += -(long)(p) & ((align) - 1); } while(0)

#define LIKELY(cond) \
  __builtin_expect((cond), 1)

#define UNLIKELY(cond) \
  __builtin_expect((cond), 0)

#define CACHE_LINE_ALIGNED \
  __attribute__ ((aligned(CHIP_L1D_LINE_SIZE())))


#define VERIFY_GXIO(VAL, WHAT)                                  \
  do {                                                          \
    long long __val = (long) (VAL);                             \
    if (__val < 0)                                              \
      tmc_task_die("%s: (%lld) %s.",                            \
                   (WHAT), __val, gxio_strerror(__val));        \
  } while (0)

#define VERIFY_ERRNO(VAL, WHAT)                                 \
  do {                                                          \
    long long __val = (long) (VAL);                             \
    if (__val == -1)                                            \
      tmc_task_die("%s: (%lld) %s.",                            \
                   (WHAT), __val, strerror(errno));             \
  } while (0)


#define DELAY(COUNT)                                            \
  do {                                                          \
    for (int i=0; i < COUNT; ++i)                               \
      __insn_mfspr(SPR_PASS);                                   \
  } while (0)


#define FLUSH_EQUEUE(EQUEUE)                                    \
  do {                                                          \
    gxio_mpipe_edesc_t ns = {{ .ns = 1 }};                      \
    unsigned long entries = (EQUEUE->mask_num_entries+1);       \
    for (int i = 0; i < entries; ++i)                           \
      gxio_mpipe_equeue_put(EQUEUE, ns);                        \
  } while (0)


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// globals
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Command-line options:

static char* link_name = NULL;

static int num_packets = 1000;

static unsigned int num_workers = 1;

static bool swap_mac_addresses;

static bool allow_jumbo_packets;


// Internals:

static cpu_set_t dataplane_cpu_set;

static tmc_sync_barrier_t sync_barrier;

static tmc_spin_barrier_t spin_barrier;

// To force a shared variable to occupy a single cache line,
// and thus prevent false sharing with other variables:
// - align a struct field to the cache line size (64 bytes on TILE-Gx)
// - use the struct's implicit alignment/padding behavior
//     to reserve the remainder of the same cache line.
// - use a macro to make the struct field look like the variable we want.

#define total cache_line_1.total_
static struct {
  volatile unsigned long total_ CACHE_LINE_ALIGNED;
} cache_line_1 = { 0 };

#define done cache_line_2.done_
static struct {
  volatile bool done_ CACHE_LINE_ALIGNED;
} cache_line_2 = { false };


// mPIPE data structures:

static int mpipe_instance;

static gxio_mpipe_context_t context_body;
static gxio_mpipe_context_t* const mpipe_context = &context_body;

static gxio_mpipe_iqueue_t** iqueues;

static unsigned int iqueue_entries = GXIO_MPIPE_IQUEUE_ENTRY_512;

static gxio_mpipe_equeue_t equeue_body;
static gxio_mpipe_equeue_t* const equeue = &equeue_body;

static unsigned int equeue_entries = GXIO_MPIPE_EQUEUE_ENTRY_2K;


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// create_buffer_stack()
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static void
create_buffer_stack(gxio_mpipe_context_t* context,
                    int stack_id,
                    gxio_mpipe_buffer_size_enum_t buffer_size,
                    int num_buffers)
{
  int result;

  // Buffer stack memory must be 64K byte-aligned,
  // so put it at start of a page, followed by packet buffer memory.

  // Actual space needed for buffer stack.
  size_t stack_bytes =
    gxio_mpipe_calc_buffer_stack_bytes(num_buffers);

  // Packet buffer memory must be 128-byte aligned, so add padding if needed.
  size_t needed_bytes = stack_bytes;
  ALIGN(needed_bytes, 128);

  // Convert buffer size enum to bytes.
  size_t buffer_bytes =
    gxio_mpipe_buffer_size_enum_to_buffer_size(buffer_size);

  // Total memory needed for the stack plus the buffers.
  needed_bytes += num_buffers * buffer_bytes;

  // Define buffer memory properties:
  tmc_alloc_t alloc = TMC_ALLOC_INIT;

  // - The default homing model is hash-for-home,
  //   which is what we want for buffer stacks and buffers.

  // - We have 16 IOTLB page entries available per buffer stack.
  //   To make the best use of them, and be scalable to any buffer size,
  //   we divide the the total space needed by 16 pieces
  //   and then request the smallest available page size
  //   that one piece fits into.
  tmc_alloc_set_pagesize(&alloc, (needed_bytes + 15) / 16); // ceiling(needed/16)

  //   Get the actual page size assigned above,
  //   and recalculate the actual number of pages and resulting allocation.
  //   This might be fewer than 16 pages, depending on the page size we got.
  size_t pagesize = tmc_alloc_get_pagesize(&alloc);
  int pages = (needed_bytes + pagesize - 1) / pagesize;
  int total_allocation = pages * pagesize;

  // Allocate memory pages.
  void* mem = tmc_alloc_map(&alloc, total_allocation);
  if (mem == NULL)
    tmc_task_die("create_buffer_stack(): "
                 "Could not allocate pages for buffer stack.");

  // Initialize buffer stack, associating it with memory region.
  result = gxio_mpipe_init_buffer_stack(mpipe_context, stack_id, buffer_size,
                                        mem, stack_bytes, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_init_buffer_stack()");

  // Register pages containing buffer memory with IOTLB.
  // (The buffer stack happens to be located at the start of
  //  the first page -- this is not a requirement.)
  for (int i=0; i < pages; ++i)
  {
    result = gxio_mpipe_register_page(mpipe_context, stack_id,
                                      mem + i * pagesize, pagesize, 0);
    VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_register_page()");
  }

  // Populate stack with references to buffer memory.
  mem += stack_bytes;
  for (int i=0; i < num_buffers; ++i)
  {
    gxio_mpipe_push_buffer(mpipe_context, stack_id, mem);
    mem += buffer_bytes;
  }
}


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// main_worker()
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

static void*
main_worker(void* arg)
{
  int result;

  // Thread ID, assigned by parent thread.
  int rank = (long) arg;

  // --- worker thread initialization ---

  // Bind to single dataplane cpu.
  int dataplane_cpu = tmc_cpus_find_nth_cpu(&dataplane_cpu_set, rank);
  result = tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu(dataplane_cpu);
  VERIFY_ERRNO(result, "tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu()");

  // Lock thread's current virtual pages (code, data structures, etc.)
  // into physical memory, to minimize swapping overhead.
  mlockall(MCL_CURRENT);

  // Use lock-based barrier to ensure all workers have reached this point.
  tmc_sync_barrier_wait(&sync_barrier);

  // Note: from now on, we should only use spin barriers,
  // to avoid kernel calls, locking, etc. on dataplane tiles.

  // Ensure all workers have returned from the lock and/or kernel.
  tmc_spin_barrier_wait(&spin_barrier);

#ifdef DEBUG_DATAPLANE
  // Put ZOL tiles in "debug" mode -- if interrupts other than page faults
  // occur on this tile, a kernel stacktrace is generated.
  result = set_dataplane(DP_DEBUG);
  VERIFY_ERRNO(result, "set_dataplane()");

  // Ensure all workers have successfully changed dataplane mode.
  tmc_spin_barrier_wait(&spin_barrier);
#endif

  if (rank == 0)
  {
    // Wait a few cycles, so we know everyone's through the spin barrier.
    DELAY(10000);

    // On the simulator, this enables simulation of network traffic.
    // On hardware, this is a no-op.
    sim_enable_mpipe_links(mpipe_instance, -1);
  }


  // --- forward packets ---

  // The ingress queue monitored by this worker.
  gxio_mpipe_iqueue_t* iqueue = iqueues[rank];

  // For efficiency, keep a local copy of total packet count.
  // This lives on the stack and is cached on same tile as worker.
  // It's synchronized with the global total only after each batch
  // of ingressed packets is processed, to reduce sharing/coherence overhead.
  unsigned long local_total = 0;

#ifdef DEBUG_PACKET_CYCLES
  unsigned long cycles_last  = 0;
  unsigned long cycles_now   = 0;
  unsigned long cycles_delta = 0;
  unsigned long cycles_total = 0;
  unsigned long cycles_idle  = 0;
  unsigned long avg_packet_cycles = 0;
#endif

#ifdef DEBUG_INGRESS_QUEUE
  unsigned int avg_ingress_queue_size = 0.0;
  unsigned int avg_ingress_batch_size = 0.0;
#endif

#ifdef DEBUG_PACKET_CYCLES
  // Initialize this just before we enter main loop.
  cycles_last  = get_cycle_count();
#endif

  // Keep processing packets until we hit the limit (if any).
  while (LIKELY(local_total < num_packets || num_packets <= 0))
  {
    // Input descriptors.
    gxio_mpipe_idesc_t* idescs;

    // Spin-wait for packet(s) to arrive in our queue.
    int n = gxio_mpipe_iqueue_try_peek(iqueue, &idescs);
    if (UNLIKELY(n <= 0)) // queue is currently empty
    {
      // Since we only update local_total from the global value when
      // we've actually processed packets, we use the "done" flag to
      // allow any worker to signal to the others that it's reached
      // the limit, and hence everyone else can stop too.
      if (done) break;

#ifdef DEBUG_PACKET_CYCLES
      cycles_now   = get_cycle_count();
      cycles_delta = cycles_now - cycles_last;

      // Don't count idle cycles until packets start flowing.
      if (cycles_total > 0) {
        cycles_total += cycles_delta;
        cycles_idle  += cycles_delta;
      }

      // Omit above calculations from timing.
      cycles_last  = get_cycle_count();
#endif

      continue;
    }

#ifdef DEBUG_INGRESS_QUEUE
    // calculate moving average (avg * 15 + new) / 16 without floating-point
    avg_ingress_queue_size =
      ((avg_ingress_queue_size << 4) - avg_ingress_queue_size + n) >> 4;
#endif

    // Limit packet consumption to specified batch size.
    if (n > PACKET_BATCH_SIZE)
      n = PACKET_BATCH_SIZE;

#ifdef DEBUG_INGRESS_QUEUE
    // calculate moving average (avg * 15 + new) / 16 without floating-point
    avg_ingress_batch_size =
      ((avg_ingress_batch_size << 4) - avg_ingress_batch_size + n) >> 4;
#endif

    // Prefetch idescs into cache.
    tmc_mem_prefetch(idescs, n * sizeof(gxio_mpipe_idesc_t));

    // NOTE: In general you should only prefetch what you can reasonably use
    // before it gets kicked out of cache (e.g. prefetch 1-2 packets ahead).
    // In this example, we're limiting ourselves to at most a small batch
    // of descriptors which together will easily fit in a way of the cache
    // even allowing for other activity, so we just prefetch them all.

    // Output descriptors.
    gxio_mpipe_edesc_t edescs[PACKET_BATCH_SIZE];

    for (int i=0; i < n; ++i)
    {
      gxio_mpipe_idesc_t* idesc = &idescs[i];
      gxio_mpipe_edesc_t* edesc = &edescs[i];

      // Copy packet properties to edesc.
      gxio_mpipe_edesc_copy_idesc(edesc, idesc);

      // Drop packets with MAC, truncation, CRC, or buffer errors.
      // Ignore checksum problems, if any.
      if (idesc->me || idesc->tr || idesc->ce || idesc->be)
        edesc->ns = 1; // no send

      // Rather than explicitly calling gxio_mpipe_iqueue_drop()
      // to return the packet buffer to its stack, which takes time
      // away from packet processing, we post any unwanted packet buffers
      // to the egress queue as usual, but with the "no send" flag set,
      // so the packets are skipped and their buffers automatically
      // returned to the appropriate buffer stack by the egress mechanism.
    }

    // If requested, swap dest/src MAC address fields.
    if (swap_mac_addresses)
    {
      // Prefetch packet data into cache (for non-dropped packets).
      // Again, we're fetching a small subset of the data for a limited
      // batch of packets, so it's reasonable to just prefetch everything.
      for (int i=0; i < n; ++i)
      {
        if (!edescs[i].ns)
        {
          // We only need first 12 bytes (dest/src MAC addresses).
          void* l2_start  = gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_l2_start(&idescs[i]);
          tmc_mem_prefetch(l2_start, 12);
        }
      }

      // Swap the MAC addresses.
      for (int i=0; i < n; ++i)
      {
        if (!edescs[i].ns)
        {
          void* l2_start = gxio_mpipe_idesc_get_l2_start(&idescs[i]);

          // Flip source/dest mac addresses.
          unsigned char tmac[6];
          void* dmac = l2_start + 0;
          void* smac = l2_start + 6;
          memcpy(tmac, dmac, 6);
          memcpy(dmac, smac, 6);
          memcpy(smac, tmac, 6);
        }
      }

      // Memory fence to ensure writes are visible to mPIPE before egress.
      __insn_mf();
    }

    // Reserve egress slots in batches for efficiency.
    // We don't need to know when packets have actually egressed,
    // so we can use "fast" version of function to reserve n slots.
    long slot = gxio_mpipe_equeue_reserve_fast(equeue, n);

    // Note: The reserve may spin until enough egress slots
    // are available, but for this example that's okay.

    // One could instead use the "try" version of the reserve function
    // for a non-blocking check, but that would require revising the loop
    // to do something useful while spinning,
    // like detecting/handling more packets from the ingress queue.

    // Copy egress descriptors, and release them for egress.
    for (int i=0; i < n; ++i)
      gxio_mpipe_equeue_put_at(equeue, edescs[i], slot + i);

    // Flag the input descriptors as "consumed" to free up input queue space
    // and decrement reference count of bucket(s) that delivered the packets.
    for (int i=0; i < n; ++i)
      gxio_mpipe_iqueue_consume(iqueue, &idescs[i]);

    // Count packets (atomically). The local_total is the new global total
    // after the atomic add. Note: atomic operations happen in L3 on the
    // home tile, so there is no explicit local load of the global total.
    local_total = arch_atomic_add(&total, n) + n;

#ifdef DEBUG_PACKET_CYCLES
    cycles_now   = get_cycle_count();
    cycles_delta = cycles_now - cycles_last;

    // Note: this implicitly starts "idle" cycle counting above.
    cycles_total += cycles_delta;

    // Use "busy" cycles to calculate per-packet timing.
    // Calculate moving average (avg * 15 + new) / 16 without floating-point.
    unsigned long per_packet_cycles_delta = cycles_delta / n;
    avg_packet_cycles = ((avg_packet_cycles << 4) - avg_packet_cycles
                         + per_packet_cycles_delta) >> 4;

    // Omit above calculations from timing.
    cycles_last = get_cycle_count();
#endif
  }

  if (! done)
  {
    // Let other workers still spinning waiting for packets
    // know that they can stop now.
    done = true;

    // Memory fence to ensure done flag change is visible to other workers.
    __insn_mf();
  }

  // Ensure all workers have reached this point.
  tmc_spin_barrier_wait(&spin_barrier);

#ifdef DEBUG_DATAPLANE
  // Take ZOL tiles out of "debug" mode.
  result = set_dataplane(0);
  VERIFY_ERRNO(result, "set_dataplane()");

  // Ensure all workers have successfully changed dataplane mode.
  tmc_spin_barrier_wait(&spin_barrier);
#endif

  // Unlock virtual pages.
  munlockall();

#ifdef DEBUG_PACKET_CYCLES
  printf("Worker %i, total_packets = %lu (this worker = %lu), cycles = %lu, busy = %lu, (%5.1f %%), idle = %lu (%5.1f %%)\n",
         rank,
         total,
         local_total,
         cycles_total,
         (cycles_total - cycles_idle),
         (cycles_total - cycles_idle) * 100.0 / cycles_total,
         cycles_idle,
         cycles_idle * 100.0 / cycles_total);

  printf("Worker %i, average cycles/packet = %lu\n",
         rank,
         avg_packet_cycles);
#endif

#ifdef DEBUG_INGRESS_QUEUE
  printf("Worker %i, avg queue size = %i, avg batch size = %i\n",
         rank,
         avg_ingress_queue_size,
         avg_ingress_batch_size);
#endif

  return (void*)NULL;
}


// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// main()
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

int
main(int argc, char** argv)
{
  int result;

  // --- command line ---

  // Set default link name based on whether we're on simulator or hardware.
  if (sim_is_simulator())
  {
    // On simulator, default to first simulated network link.
    link_name = "gbe0";
  }
  else
  {
    // On hardware, default to first GBE network link.
    link_name = "gbe1";
  }

  // Parse command-line arguments.
  for (int i=1; i < argc; ++i)
  {
    char* arg = argv[i];

    // --link <link_name>, used for both ingress and egress.
    if (!strcmp(arg, "--link") && i + 1 < argc)
    {
      link_name = argv[++i];
    }

    // -n packets -- maximum packets to process
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "-n") && i + 1 < argc)
    {
      num_packets = atoi(argv[++i]);
    }

    // -w workers -- number of parallel workers
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "-w") && i + 1 < argc)
    {
      num_workers = atoi(argv[++i]);
    }

    // --flip -- swap dest/src MAC addresses on egressed packets
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "--flip"))
    {
      swap_mac_addresses = true;
    }

    // --jumbo -- allow network link to accept jumbo packets
    else if (!strcmp(arg, "--jumbo"))
    {
      allow_jumbo_packets = true;
    }

    else
    {
      tmc_task_die("main(): "
                   "Unrecognized option '%s'", arg);
    }
  }


  // --- CPU availability check ---

  // Get the current dataplane cpu set.
  result = tmc_cpus_get_dataplane_cpus(&dataplane_cpu_set);

  // Verify enough tiles are running in ZOL (dataplane) mode.
  VERIFY_ERRNO(result, "tmc_cpus_get_dataplane_cpus()");
  if (tmc_cpus_count(&dataplane_cpu_set) < num_workers)
    tmc_task_die("main(): "
                 "Insufficient dataplane cpus (%d) "
                 "for number of workers (%d).",
                 tmc_cpus_count(&dataplane_cpu_set), num_workers);


  // --- initialize mPIPE ---

  // ingress/egress link, on single mPIPE instance:

  // Get the mPIPE instance associated with the network link.
  // This also implicitly validates that the specified link exists.
  mpipe_instance = gxio_mpipe_link_instance(link_name);
  if (mpipe_instance < 0)
    tmc_task_die("main(): "
                 "Network link '%s' does not exist.", link_name);

  // Initialize the driver for the mPIPE instance.
  result = gxio_mpipe_init(mpipe_context, mpipe_instance);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_init()");

  // Open the link.
  // Note: we assume the link is already up and don't wait for it.
  gxio_mpipe_link_t link;
  result = gxio_mpipe_link_open(&link,
                                mpipe_context, link_name, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_link_open()");

  // Get channel number associated with link.
  // In this example, we'll only use it for egress queue.
  // We won't for example, classify incoming packets by channel.
  int link_channel = gxio_mpipe_link_channel(&link);

  // If requested, allow the link to receive jumbo packets.
  if (allow_jumbo_packets)
    gxio_mpipe_link_set_attr(&link, GXIO_MPIPE_LINK_RECEIVE_JUMBO, 1);


  // --- ingress queue (NotifRing and iqueue) ---

  // Allocate NotifRing IDs, one per parallel worker.
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_rings(mpipe_context, num_workers, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_rings()");

  // First ring ID:
  unsigned int ring_id = result; 

  // Determine memory needed for each NotifRing and its iqueue wrapper.
  size_t notif_ring_size = iqueue_entries * sizeof(gxio_mpipe_idesc_t);
  size_t needed_bytes = notif_ring_size + sizeof(gxio_mpipe_iqueue_t);

  // Array of pointers to iqueue wrappers, one per worker.
  // Workers only read this array once,
  // so it doesn't require special memory allocation.
  iqueues = calloc(num_workers, sizeof(gxio_mpipe_iqueue_t*));
  if (iqueues == NULL)
    tmc_task_die("Failed to allocate iqueues array.");

  // Allocate a page of memory for each NotifRing and its iqueue wrapper.
  // The NotifRing must be 4K aligned, so we put it at the start of the page.
  for (int i=0; i < num_workers; ++i)
  {
    // Define memory properties:
    tmc_alloc_t alloc = TMC_ALLOC_INIT;
    
    // - NotifRing memory page is homed on same tile as worker.
    tmc_alloc_set_home(&alloc,
      tmc_cpus_find_nth_cpu(&dataplane_cpu_set, i));

    // - The NotifRing must use physically contiguous memory,
    //   but the iqueue following it is allowed to span pages,
    //   so we use "notif_ring_size", not "needed_bytes" when requesting
    //   the page size, to get the smallest actual page size
    //   that at least fits the NotifRing.
    tmc_alloc_set_pagesize(&alloc, notif_ring_size);

    // Allocate memory page(s).
    void* iqueue_mem = tmc_alloc_map(&alloc, needed_bytes);
    if (iqueue_mem == NULL)
      tmc_task_die("main(): "
                   "Failed to allocate memory for NotifRing %i.", i);

    // Ingress queue data structure is located after ring buffer.
    gxio_mpipe_iqueue_t* iqueue = iqueue_mem + notif_ring_size;

    // Initialize iqueue and NotifRing, and associate memory with them.
    result = gxio_mpipe_iqueue_init(iqueue, mpipe_context, ring_id + i,
                                    iqueue_mem, notif_ring_size, 0);
    VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_iqueue_init()");
    iqueues[i] = iqueue;
  }


  // --- NotifGroup and buckets ---

  // We don't need to differentiate ingress queues/workers,
  // so we only need a single notifgroup and set of buckets for this example.

  // Allocate a single NotifGroup.
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_groups(mpipe_context, 1, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_notif_groups()");

  // First NotifGroup ID:
  unsigned int group_id = result;

  // Allocate a single set of buckets.

  // The default classifier requires the number of buckets be a power of 2,
  // but the specific number depends on the load-balancing being used.
  int num_buckets = 1024; 
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_buckets(mpipe_context, num_buckets, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_buckets()");

  // First bucket ID:
  unsigned int bucket_id = result;

  // We're just forwarding packets as-is, so use round-robin load-balancing.
  gxio_mpipe_bucket_mode_t mode = GXIO_MPIPE_BUCKET_ROUND_ROBIN;

  // If we care about packet ordering, or were looking at entire flows,
  // we'd want to use dynamic flow affinity instead, i.e.:
  // gxio_mpipe_bucket_mode_t mode = GXIO_MPIPE_BUCKET_DYNAMIC_FLOW_AFFINITY.

  // Initialize group and buckets, assign initial NotifRing to each bucket
  // (round-robin from the specified ring ID range),
  // and set the load-balancing mode for the group.
  result = gxio_mpipe_init_notif_group_and_buckets(mpipe_context,
                                                   group_id,
                                                   ring_id, num_workers,
                                                   bucket_id, num_buckets,
                                                   mode);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_init_notif_group_and_buckets()");


  // --- egress queue (eDMA ring and equeue) ---

  // Allocate eDMA ring IDs.
  // Note: This example uses a single ring only for simplicity.
  // This is not a requirement.
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_edma_rings(mpipe_context, 1, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_edma_rings");

  // First eDMA ring ID
  unsigned int ering_id = result;

  // Determine memory needed for eDMA ring.
  size_t ering_size = equeue_entries * sizeof(gxio_mpipe_edesc_t);

  // Allocate page of memory for eDMA ring.
  // The eDMA ring must be 32K aligned, so we put it at the start of a page.

  // Define memory properties:
  tmc_alloc_t edescs_alloc = TMC_ALLOC_INIT;

  // - Default cache line homing is hash-for-home,
  //   which is appropriate for eDMA ring and equeue.

  // - We're storing the equeue structure separately from 
  //   the eDMA ring, since there's only one, so we just need
  //   to request a page large enough for the eDMA ring.
  tmc_alloc_set_pagesize(&edescs_alloc, ering_size);

  // Allocate the page:
  void* edescs = tmc_alloc_map(&edescs_alloc, ering_size);
  if (edescs == NULL)
    tmc_task_die("Failed to allocate equeue memory.");

  // Initialize equeue and eDMA ring, and associate memory with them.
  // Also, specify the network link channel this equeue is associated with.
  result = gxio_mpipe_equeue_init(equeue, mpipe_context,
                                  ering_id, link_channel,
                                  edescs, ering_size, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_gxio_equeue_init()");


  // --- buffer stacks and buffers ---

  // We're using two (or three) buffer stacks,
  // to support small and large (and jumbo, if enabled) packet sizes.

  // Use sufficient buffers on small/large stacks to avoid ever getting
  // buffer errors (idesc->be == 1) even with completely full queues.
  unsigned int num_bufs = equeue_entries + num_workers * iqueue_entries;

  // Allocate buffer stack IDs:
  result = gxio_mpipe_alloc_buffer_stacks(mpipe_context, 
                                          allow_jumbo_packets ? 3 : 2, 0, 0);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_alloc_buffer_stacks()");

  // Initial buffer stack ID:
  unsigned int stack_id = result;

  // Create and populate small buffer stack.
  create_buffer_stack(mpipe_context, stack_id + 0,
                      GXIO_MPIPE_BUFFER_SIZE_128, num_bufs);

  // Create and populate large buffer stack.
  create_buffer_stack(mpipe_context, stack_id + 1,
                      GXIO_MPIPE_BUFFER_SIZE_1664, num_bufs);

  if (allow_jumbo_packets)
  {
    // Create and populate jumbo buffer stack.

    // Here we use 16K buffers, because 4K buffers are too small,
    // and 10K buffers can induce "false chaining" for huge packets.
    // We use only 4 buffers per worker, because the buffers consume
    // a lot of memory, and the risk of buffer errors (idesc->be==1) is low.
    create_buffer_stack(mpipe_context, stack_id + 2,
                        GXIO_MPIPE_BUFFER_SIZE_16384, num_workers * 4);

    // Increase egress queue "store and forward" size to guarantee that
    // the largest possible jumbo packet size can be egressed safely.
    result = gxio_mpipe_equeue_set_snf_size(equeue, 10384);
    VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_equeue_set_snf_size()");
  }


  // --- default classifier rules ---

  // Define a single rule that applies to all packets.
  gxio_mpipe_rules_t rules;
  gxio_mpipe_rules_init(&rules, mpipe_context);
  gxio_mpipe_rules_begin(&rules, bucket_id, num_buckets, NULL);
  result = gxio_mpipe_rules_commit(&rules);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_rules_commit()");


  // --- create parallel workers ---

  // Barriers used to synchronize worker creation.
  tmc_sync_barrier_init(&sync_barrier, num_workers);
  tmc_spin_barrier_init(&spin_barrier, num_workers);

  // Spin off worker threads.
  pthread_t threads[num_workers];
  for (int i=1; i < num_workers; ++i)
  {
    if (pthread_create(&threads[i], NULL, main_worker, 
                       (void*)(intptr_t)i) != 0)
      tmc_task_die("main(): Failed to create worker thread %i.", i);
  }

  // Initial thread also becomes a worker.
  (void) main_worker((void*)(intptr_t)0);

  // Wait for other threads to exit.
  for (int i=1; i < num_workers; ++i)
  {
    if (pthread_join(threads[i], NULL) != 0)
      tmc_task_die("main(): Join failed for worker thread %i", i);
  }

  // Now that we know no workers are egressing new traffic,
  // ensure any pending egress traffic has completed.
  FLUSH_EQUEUE(equeue);


  // --- cleanup ---

  // Close/release mPIPE hardware resources.
  result = gxio_mpipe_link_close(&link);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_link_close()");

  result = gxio_mpipe_destroy(mpipe_context);
  VERIFY_GXIO(result, "gxio_mpipe_destroy()");

  // There's no need to "release" context-managed state,
  // such as mPIPE ring/buffer IDs, IOTLB entries, etc.

  // In a long-running app, one would also free memory allocations
  // for rings, buffers, queues, etc.
  // but we're exiting so we'll just let these drop.

  return 0;
}

All other trademarks and/or registered trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

Third-party software: The Tilera IDE makes use of the BeanShell scripting library. Source code for the BeanShell library can be found at the BeanShell website (http://www.beanshell.org/developer.html).

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).
